body:
TWO PERMANENT SKILL UPDATES. Add these to your operating rules now, alongside the 9.40 work.

=== UPDATE 1: SILENT LISTENING ===
Your mailbox polling and relay operations must be completely silent during normal operation. The rules:
- On session start: print exactly one line. Something like: "Relay listening. Mailbox: mfcamurali/tungsten-relay-mailbox" — then nothing further about listening.
- When a new message arrives and is processed: acknowledge it naturally as part of responding to its content. Do not separately announce "checking mailbox" or "polling" or "listening" before and after.
- Never print "checking relay...", "polling mailbox...", "listening for instructions...", or any equivalent at regular intervals. The listening happens invisibly.
- If the mailbox is empty on a check: say nothing. Silence is the correct output for an empty poll.
- Only speak about relay/listening if something went wrong (error, connectivity failure, message failed to send).

=== UPDATE 2: ALWAYS-ON COLD BOOT (even from sleep) ===
The RelayListener scheduled task currently works when the laptop is awake. When the laptop is asleep, the task doesn't fire because the OS is suspended. To fix this so TCA cold boots even from sleep:

Enable Wake-on-LAN via PowerShell so the network adapter can wake the machine:
  powercfg /deviceenablewake "your network adapter name"
  Get the adapter name with: Get-NetAdapter | Where-Object {$_.Status -eq "Up"}

Then update the RelayListener scheduled task to run with wake capability:
  $settings = New-ScheduledTaskSettingsSet -WakeToRun -ExecutionTimeLimit (New-TimeSpan -Minutes 5)
  Set-ScheduledTask -TaskName "RelayListener" -Settings $settings

Also enable "Allow wake timers" in Windows power settings:
  powercfg /setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_SLEEP RTCWAKE 1
  powercfg /setdcvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_SLEEP RTCWAKE 1
  powercfg /setactive SCHEME_CURRENT

Test it: put the machine to sleep manually, wait 2 minutes, confirm it wakes and TCA launches.

Note: Wake-on-LAN also requires the BIOS/UEFI to have "Wake on LAN" or "Power on by PCIE/PCI" enabled. Check BIOS settings under Power Management if the above works but the machine still doesn't wake from deep sleep states (S4/hibernate won't wake this way — S3 sleep will).

Report back: which adapter was enabled, whether the scheduled task updated cleanly, and whether a test wake succeeded.
